models/marts/order_items.yml.purpose: This file defines the data model and business metrics for order items, enabling analysis of revenue breakdown by food vs drink items, cost calculations, and profit metrics. It serves as a semantic layer for order item analytics.

Taskfile.yml.purpose: Defines and orchestrates the complete data workflow for the jaffle shop analytics project, including environment setup, data generation, and database loading processes.

macros/cents_to_dollars.sql.purpose: Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms for consistent financial reporting.

models/marts/orders.sql.purpose: This mart model aggregates order data with item-level details to create a comprehensive view of each order, including cost summaries, item counts, and customer ordering sequence. It enhances raw order data with business-relevant computed fields like whether an order contains food or drink items and the customer's order history rank.

models/marts/supplies.yml.purpose: Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business intelligence reporting on supply and product combinations, including supply characteristics like cost and perishability.

models/staging/stg_orders.yml.purpose: Maintains cleaned and validated order data with financial integrity checks, providing a reliable foundation for order-based business analysis and reporting.

macros/generate_schema_name.sql.purpose: Manages database schema organization by defining rules for where different types of data assets (seeds, models) should be deployed based on environment and naming conventions.

models/marts/orders.yml.purpose: This file defines the 'orders' data mart, which provides a comprehensive view of order data including financial details, item types, and customer information. It serves as a centralized source for order-related analytics and reporting.

models/marts/customers.yml.purpose: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer-level metrics including lifetime spending, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a centralized source for customer analytics and supports downstream metrics like average order value and lifetime value calculations.

models/marts/customers.sql.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that aggregates lifetime order history and spending patterns to classify customers as new or returning, enabling customer segmentation and retention analysis.

models/staging/stg_order_items.yml.purpose: Represents individual food and drink items that compose customer orders, enabling detailed order analysis and relationship tracking between order items and their parent orders.

models/marts/order_items.sql.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to include product details, order timestamps, and supply costs. It aggregates supply costs per product and provides a unified dataset for downstream reporting and analysis.

models/staging/stg_products.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units, and adds boolean flags to categorize products as food or drink items.

models/marts/locations.yml.purpose: Establishes the location dimension for semantic modeling with standardized location attributes and a measure for average tax rate, enabling location-based business intelligence and financial analysis.

models/staging/__sources.yml.purpose: Establishes the foundational data source definitions and metadata documentation for the e-commerce analytics system, mapping raw database tables to business entities.

models/staging/stg_orders.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollars, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

models/marts/products.yml.purpose: Defines the product dimension for semantic modeling, providing standardized product attributes and categorizations to support consistent product-based metrics and analysis across the business.

models/marts/metricflow_time_spine.sql.purpose: Generates a comprehensive date spine for time-series analysis and reporting across business metrics, though appears to be unused in current implementations.

models/staging/stg_supplies.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format with cleaned column names, calculated fields, and appropriate data types for downstream analytics use.

models/staging/stg_locations.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames fields from the source while adding basic transformations like date truncation.

models/staging/stg_customers.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data from the ecom source by renaming fields to standardized column names. It selects only the customer ID and name, preparing the data for downstream usage in the analytics stack.

models/staging/stg_order_items.sql.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields. It prepares granular order item records for downstream analytics by mapping raw identifiers to business-friendly column names.

models/staging/stg_locations.yml.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized list of open locations for business analysis, with each row representing a unique location and including key attributes like location ID, name, tax rate, and opening date.

models/staging/stg_supplies.yml.purpose: Tracks supply expense data with basic cleaning and transformation, maintaining one row per supply cost (not per supply item). Multiple rows may exist per supply_id due to cost fluctuations over time.

models/staging/stg_customers.yml.purpose: Maintains cleaned and transformed customer data with one record per unique customer for consistent customer identification and management.

models/staging/stg_products.yml.purpose: Manages cleaned and transformed product data representing food and drink items available for ordering, with one record per unique product.

models/marts/products.sql.purpose: Provides a cleaned and standardized view of product data for business reporting and analysis. Enables downstream consumers to access consistent product information without needing to understand the underlying raw data structure.

models/marts/supplies.sql.purpose: Delivers a unified view of supply chain data for operational reporting and inventory management decisions. Supports business users in understanding supply availability and relationships to products.

dbt_project.yml.purpose: This dbt project configuration file defines the structure and settings for the 'jaffle_shop' analytics project, including model paths, deployment targets, and data transformation rules.

.pre-commit-config.yaml.purpose: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that enforce code quality standards and formatting rules before commits are accepted. It automates validation checks for YAML syntax, file formatting, and Python code style.

models/marts/locations.sql.purpose: Provides a simplified view of location data by selecting all records from the staging locations model without additional transformation.

Order Analytics & Revenue.description: Core business domain focused on order processing, revenue calculation, and financial analysis of customer purchases including food/drink breakdowns, cost calculations, and profit metrics.

Customer Analytics & Segmentation.description: Domain focused on customer behavior analysis, lifetime value calculation, segmentation, and retention metrics derived from order history and spending patterns.

Product & Supply Chain Management.description: Domain managing product catalog data, supply chain information, inventory relationships, and product categorization for operational reporting and analysis.

Location & Tax Analysis.description: Domain focused on location dimension modeling, store data management, and tax rate analysis for location-based business intelligence.

Data Infrastructure & Governance.description: Cross-cutting domain managing data pipeline orchestration, schema management, source definitions, and project configuration.

Time Series & Metric Foundation.description: Supporting domain providing time-series analysis infrastructure and date dimension for metric reporting across business domains.

business_logic_hotspots.purpose: This file defines the data model and business metrics for order items, enabling analysis of revenue breakdown by food vs drink items, cost calculations, and profit metrics. It serves as a semantic layer for order item analytics.
Defines and orchestrates the complete data workflow for the jaffle shop analytics project, including environment setup, data generation, and database loading processes.
Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms for consistent financial reporting.

This mart model aggregates order data with item-level details to create a comprehensive view of each order, including cost summaries, item counts, and customer ordering sequence. It enhances raw order data with business-relevant computed fields like whether an order contains food or drink items and the customer's order history rank.
Defines the supplies semantic model to enable business intelligence reporting on supply and product combinations, including supply characteristics like cost and perishability.
Maintains cleaned and validated order data with financial integrity checks, providing a reliable foundation for order-based business analysis and reporting.
Manages database schema organization by defining rules for where different types of data assets (seeds, models) should be deployed based on environment and naming conventions.

This file defines the 'orders' data mart, which provides a comprehensive view of order data including financial details, item types, and customer information. It serves as a centralized source for order-related analytics and reporting.

This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates key customer-level metrics including lifetime spending, order counts, and customer type classification. It serves as a centralized source for customer analytics and supports downstream metrics like average order value and lifetime value calculations.

Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that aggregates lifetime order history and spending patterns to classify customers as new or returning, enabling customer segmentation and retention analysis.

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format suitable for analytics. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units, and adds boolean flags to categorize products as food or drink items.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming (sku→product_id, name→product_name) and transformation (cents_to_dollars macro on price field)
Business rule implementation using coalesce to create boolean flags for product types
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_products and writes model.stg_products

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting cent values to dollars, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity for downstream analytics use.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming (id→order_id, store_id→location_id, customer→customer_id) and currency conversions using {{cents_to_dollars}} macro
Timestamp normalization using {{dbt.date_trunc('day','ordered_at')}}
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_orders and writes model.stg_orders

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the ecom source into a standardized format with cleaned column names, calculated fields, and appropriate data types for downstream analytics use.

supporting_evidence.description: generation of surrogate key and renaming of id/sku columns
cents_to_dollars macro application on cost field
boolean field transformation for perishable supplies

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This mart model aggregates order data with item-level details to create a comprehensive view of each order, including cost summaries, item counts, and customer ordering sequence. It enhances raw order data with business-relevant computed fields like whether an order contains food or drink items and the customer's order history rank.

supporting_evidence.description: CTE 'order_items_summary' computes aggregated metrics such as supply cost, product price subtotal, and categorized item counts (food/drink) per order
CTE 'compute_booleans' joins order data with summary stats and derives boolean flags for food and drink orders
CTE 'customer_order_count' assigns sequential numbering to orders by customer, indicating order history position

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This model creates a comprehensive view of order items by joining staging tables for orders, products, and supplies to include product details, order timestamps, and supply costs. It aggregates supply costs per product and provides a unified dataset for downstream reporting and analysis.

supporting_evidence.description: Selecting core order item fields and joining with orders, products, and aggregated supply cost data
Aggregation of supply costs by product ID from the supplies staging table
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_order_items, model.stg_orders, model.stg_products and writes model.order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames fields from the source while adding basic transformations like date truncation.

supporting_evidence.description: Field renaming and transformation: id→location_id, name→location_name, tax_rate, and opened_at→opened_date
Source reference to ecom.raw_stores indicates input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_stores and writes model.stg_locations

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw customer data from the ecom source by renaming fields to standardized column names. It selects only the customer ID and name, preparing the data for downstream usage in the analytics stack.

supporting_evidence.description: id renamed to customer_id and name renamed to customer_name in SELECT clause
Source reference to ecom.raw_customers
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_customers and writes model.stg_customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce item data into a standardized format by renaming and selecting key fields. It prepares granular order item records for downstream analytics by mapping raw identifiers to business-friendly column names.

supporting_evidence.description: Column renaming logic maps raw fields (id, sku) to business concepts (order_item_id, product_id)
Source reference {{ source('ecom', 'raw_items') }} indicates input dataset
Transformation dbt_model reads source.ecom.raw_items and writes model.stg_order_items

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Creates a comprehensive customer analytics dataset that aggregates lifetime order history and spending patterns to classify customers as new or returning, enabling customer segmentation and retention analysis.

supporting_evidence.description: Aggregates customer order data including count_lifetime_orders, lifetime_spend_pretax, lifetime_tax_paid, and lifetime_spend
Classifies customers as 'returning' or 'new' based on is_repeat_buyer logic
Transformation dbt_model reads model.stg_customers, model.orders and writes model.customers

semantic_hotspots.purpose: Provides a standardized way to convert monetary values from cents to dollars across different database platforms for consistent financial reporting.

supporting_evidence.description: Macro definition with adapter-specific implementations for converting cents to dollars: default__cents_to_dollars, postgres__cents_to_dollars, bigquery__cents_to_dollars, fabric__cents_to_dollars
